nextAction: Choose the next separately reviewed macOS integration slice or return to governance work. Litmo Ops stays locked until server-backed staff authentication and authorization exist.
blockedBy: Legal/privacy/security review for destructive retention and deletion
Qualified safeguarding/legal review for external escalation
Named independent backup reviewer before external alpha and two-person permanent bans
Apple signing credentials and reviewed staging configuration for a signed device/TestFlight build
risks: Invitation membership is admission control, not identity verification, proof of safety, or consent.
The routine kill-switch pauses discovery and new requests but intentionally preserves participant safety controls and does not force-end active sessions.
Self export is an engineering portability primitive, not a claim of legally complete data access.
External TestFlight remains blocked by deletion, hosted staging, AASA, disclosures, independent review, and signed-release evidence.
Campfire circle readiness is ephemeral local facilitation, not a real group session or multi-person consent record.
The hosted Xcode lane is unsigned simulator compilation, not device, entitlement, archive, or TestFlight validation.
The native macOS participant app is local Campfire plus a read-only shell; it is not an active-session controller.
Litmo Ops is separately bundled but intentionally non-operational until real staff authentication, authorization, audit logging, and API contracts exist.
notes: PR #76 merged and adds native SwiftUI participant/Campfire and separately locked Ops targets under ADR 0045.
macOS Native Build run #2 passed after adding the required generated framework Info.plist.
Unsigned hosted artifacts are inspection builds, not signed or notarized releases.
Destructive report/case/trust retention and account deletion remain deliberately unimplemented pending review.
